steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers say they can't attribute the 40–90% production drop specifically to honeybees because the shielding may have blocked other insect pollinators too. The correct choice must supply evidence that other insects pollinate cucumbers and were also blocked.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — discusses onion pollination: Onions are a different crop and irrelevant to explaining the cucumber experiment's confound. ✗
  • Choice B — squash bees pollinate cucumber plants and the shielding blocked them: This directly establishes the confound — another pollinator was excluded alongside honeybees, so the experiment cannot isolate honeybee-specific effects. ✓
  • Choice C — discusses relative frequency of honeybee use: How often honeybees are used generally does not explain why the shielded experiment conflated multiple pollinator types. ✗
  • Choice D — addresses sunlight exposure: Sunlight is unrelated to whether the shielding blocked non-honeybee pollinators. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
